operator_scope.semantic_signature: candidate irregular-memory schedule for KBX index_select, scatter, masked_select, expand_where, or fused_gather_masked_fill after correctness passes

valid_when: Task uses explicit indices, masks, scatter writes, Boolean compaction, or broadcasted where, and a semantic candidate is hidden-correct.
Profile summary indicates irregular/uncoalesced memory, mask-density sensitivity, or scatter duplicate-index contention.
invalid_when: Access pattern is a regular affine permute_copy or contiguous pointwise operation.
Correctness for bounds, mask shape, index dtype, and duplicate-index policy has not passed.
preconditions: Apply semantic capsules for stride/index/mask/scatter first.

mechanism: Irregular KBX layout tasks have different memory legs: index loads, mask loads, source reads, destination writes, compaction positions, and optional duplicate-index writes. Optimizing the wrong leg often wastes time.

instructions: Separate index/mask loads, source payload reads, destination writes, and compaction/scatter conflict logic before tuning.
For index_select/fused_gather_masked_fill, coalesce contiguous payload columns when row payload is contiguous; do not assume adjacent indices are adjacent in memory.
For scatter, preserve overwrite vs additive semantics and benchmark duplicate-index distributions separately from unique-index distributions.
For masked_select, treat mask density as a performance variable and avoid dense output assumptions.
For expand_where, use pointwise broadcast coalescing rather than gather/scatter tiling.

expected_effect.correctness: No effect unless semantic capsules are followed; tuning may reveal missing bounds/mask/collision checks.
expected_effect.performance: Candidate-only: may improve coalescing or reduce wasted tuning; no H20 speedup is asserted.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Prevents applying regular transpose tiling or pointwise fusion blindly to irregular memory tasks.
expected_effect.risk: Index distribution and mask density can dominate; visible distribution tuning may not transfer to held-out cases.

performance_requirements: Report index distribution and mask density assumptions with benchmark results.
Do not promote from a single collision or mask-density pattern.
validation.promotion_policy: Requires H20 measurements across multiple index/mask distributions plus profile attribution.

known_failures: Assuming gather indices are sorted or contiguous can overfit public inputs.
Treating scatter overwrite as scatter-add changes semantics.
Removing bounds or mask checks for speed can create illegal memory accesses or wrong output shapes.
